seoTitle: Round Rock ISD Calendar 2026-2027: Holidays, Breaks & PDF
seoDescription: Round Rock ISD 2026-2027 calendar with Aug 19 first day, May 27 last day, holidays, breaks, student holidays, PDF and ICS.

calendarFaqs.q: Does Round Rock Independent School District have a fall break in 2026-2027?
calendarFaqs.a: Yes. Round Rock Independent School District has a fall break running from Nov 23, 2026 to Nov 27, 2026, a total of 5 days.

calendarFaqs.q: When does school start for Round Rock Independent School District in 2026-2027?
calendarFaqs.a: The first day of school for Round Rock Independent School District is Wednesday, August 19, 2026. Students and staff are expected to report on this date to begin the new academic year.

yearNumbers.label: Starts Later
yearNumbers.detail: First day moves from Aug 12 to Aug 19 — one of the largest year-over-year start date shifts among major Texas districts.

yearNumbers.label: Days Down
yearNumbers.detail: Instructional days drop from 171 to 170 vs 2025–2026, the lowest among the districts in this comparison.

yearNumbers.label: Ends Later
yearNumbers.detail: Last day moves from May 21 to May 27 vs 2025–2026, reflecting the later start.

yearNumbers.label: Diwali Removed
yearNumbers.detail: Diwali was a formal student and staff holiday in 2025–2026; it does not appear on the 2026–2027 calendar.

events.name: Last Day of Classes / First Semester Ends
Last Day of School / Second Semester Ends

calendarNotes: Round Rock ISD begins the 2026–2027 school year on August 19, 2026 — one of the later start dates among major Texas districts — and finishes May 27, 2027. First semester wraps up December 17 before Winter Break begins, so students returning January 5 start second semester immediately, without the mid-semester transition that most Texas districts schedule after the holidays. Spring Break runs March 15–19, one week later than most San Antonio-area districts. Like other recent RRISD calendars, the district logs 170 instructional days using longer school days rather than the 175–180 day schedule most Texas districts follow.
